title: Code of Conduct
text: AutoXplainR committed respectful, harassment-free community. considerate, critique ideas evidence rather people, disclose relevant conflicts interest, make space participants different levels experience. Unacceptable conduct includes harassment, discriminatory language, threats, doxxing, sustained disruption, publishing another person’s private information without permission. Report concerns privately Matteo Mazzarelli matteo.mazzarelli@gmail.com. Reports handled confidentially promptly practical. Maintainers may edit remove content, issue warnings, temporarily restrict participation, permanently exclude participants needed protect community.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: Thanks helping improve explanation reliability R. Contributions welcome issues, design discussions, documentation, tests, code.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: material API statistical-method change, open issue first. Describe: user claim method intended support; estimand assumptions; failure modes dependence, finite samples, model multiplicity; proposed object contract backward-compatibility impact; validation evidence simulation plan. Features accepted solely produce attractive chart.

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: ordinary suite must start Java, access internet, open browser, require credentials. H2O tests isolated opt-:
code: pak::pak(c("devtools", "testthat", "roxygen2", "lintr")) devtools::load_all() devtools::test() devtools::check() AUTOXPLAIN_RUN_H2O=true Rscript -e 'devtools::test(filter = "h2o")'

title: Contributing to AutoXplainR
text: Add unit tests happy paths, invalid inputs, statistical edge cases. Add deterministic simulation numerical method introduced. Preserve caller’s random-number state stochastic functions. silently drop columns, rows, factor levels. State whether intervals Monte Carlo, bootstrap, inferential. Add NEWS user documentation visible changes. Run devtools::document(), devtools::test(), devtools::check(). Keep optional packages behind requireNamespace() checks. contributions licensed project MIT license. Participation subject Code Conduct.

title: Governance
text: AutoXplainR currently maintainer-led. maintainer final responsibility releases, CRAN submissions, security responses, statistical scope, API stability. Material method changes require public design issue evidence appropriate claim: literature references, simulations, cross-package comparisons, three. second reviewer approve changes statistical estimators, remote-data boundaries, evidence-grade thresholds stable 1.0 release. Decisions favor explicit assumptions, reproducibility, honest limitations feature count. Disagreements recorded relevant issue pull request.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: 0.2 architecture establishes model-agnostic evidence audit. roadmap ordered scientific leverage, novelty theatre.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Model-agnostic prediction contract H2O boundary. Repeated grouped permutation importance retained draws. ALE, diagnosed PDP, feature association, support outputs. Supplied near-optimal-model explanation prediction agreement. Evidence-first standalone report provenance. Fast core tests, isolated H2O tests, CI, pkgdown, project policy.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Observation-level loss deltas explicitly supported inferential procedures multiplicity correction; benchmark xplainfi. Conditional-sampler interface rather treating blocked permutations general conditional estimator. Bootstrap observations refits separate permutation, evaluation-sample, model-fitting variability. Cross-fitted audit orchestration prevents training/evaluation leakage construction. Calibration, subgroup performance, missingness-shift diagnostics context explanation claims—fairness certification. Simulation corpus known ground truth, correlated features, interactions, null predictors, extrapolation, label noise.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: First-class Rashomon-set provider interface AutoML, resampling, regularization paths, user-defined candidate generators. Rashomon ALE bands class-reliance ranges coverage diagnostics. Case-level prediction ambiguity, feature-attribution disagreement, decision-threshold sensitivity. Pareto selection performance, calibration, latency, stability, explanation agreement without hiding trade-offs one weighted score.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Versioned JSON audit schema validation package CI/model registry gates. Signed report manifests model, data, code, environment hashes. Redaction policy pluggable local narrative models. WCAG audit, print/PDF stylesheet, report localization. Reproducible benchmark website comparing runtime numerical agreement DALEX, ingredients, iml, xplainfi, reference implementations.

title: AutoXplainR Roadmap
text: Stable public object schemas documented deprecation policy. Zero errors, warnings, unexplained notes across CRAN reference platforms. Independent review estimands, simulations, reporting language. least two non-H2O production adapters validated integration tests. Published methods/validation manuscript archived benchmark artifacts.

title: Security Policy
text: Security fixes applied current development branch recent released minor version.

title: Security Policy
text: open public issue suspected vulnerability accidental secret exposure. Email Matteo Mazzarelli matteo.mazzarelli@gmail.com : affected version platform; reproduction steps minimal example; potential impact; suggested mitigation. Please omit real API keys, personal data, proprietary model artifacts. receive acknowledgement within seven days.

title: Security Policy
text: Core explanation audits local access network. Remote narrative generation optional sends aggregated diagnostics. Users remain responsible provider terms data policy. API keys read arguments environment variables must never written reports, logs, fixtures, error messages. Generated HTML escapes model labels, feature names, findings, metadata.

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: Permutation importance feature-effect plots useful descriptions fitted model. also easy -interpret. feature can appear important particular random permutation, correlated predictors can make marginal perturbations unrealistic, different equally accurate model can support different story. AutoXplainR treats explanation claim needs recorded evidence contract: fitted model evaluation distribution described? numerical result survive repetition random computation? feature-dependence assumptions pressure? similarly performing supplied models support ranking? language justified diagnostics? resulting grade transparent triage heuristic. p-value, causal result, fairness assessment, certification.

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: small example uses fixed split keep vignette reproducible. real analysis design evaluation split fitting retain enough observations stable diagnostics. explain_model() validates model produces predictions. explainer stores feature data, outcomes, task metadata, lightweight provenance ID. unsupported model class, pass predict_function. Regression functions return numeric predictions; binary functions return positive-class probability; multiclass functions return named probability matrix.
code: train <- mtcars[1:24, ] test <- mtcars[25:32, ]  model_a <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = train) model_b <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = train) explainer_a <- explain_model(   model_a,   test,   y = "mpg",   label = "displacement model",   metadata = list(evaluation_role = "test") )  explainer_b <- explain_model(   model_b,   test,   y = "mpg",   label = "acceleration model",   metadata = list(evaluation_role = "test") )  explainer_a #> <AutoXplainR explainer> #>   model:    displacement model #>   task:     regression #>   data:     8 rows x 10 features #>   id:       axr-0c9b3882

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: result deliberately retains negative values. interval Monte Carlo interval across permutation repeats. Repeating algorithm create new independent observations population, population-level inference. Feature groups can permuted jointly: optional within argument permutes within explicit strata. useful blocked sensitivity analysis, advertised general conditional permutation estimator.
code: importance <- calculate_permutation_importance(   explainer_a,   features = c("wt", "hp", "disp"),   n_repeats = 10,   seed = 2026 )  importance #> <AutoXplainR permutation importance> #>   metric: rmse | repeats: 10 | baseline: 2.5436 #>  feature importance  std_error   conf_low  conf_high sign_stability #>       hp  2.5556753 0.22940656  2.0367217 3.07462904            1.0 #>       wt  0.9892010 0.21483320  0.5032146 1.47518747            0.9 #>     disp -0.1262717 0.05856004 -0.2587437 0.00620031            0.7 #>   intervals describe permutation Monte Carlo variation, not population inference calculate_permutation_importance(   explainer_a,   feature_groups = list(engine = c("hp", "disp"), weight = "wt"),   n_repeats = 5,   seed = 2026 ) #> <AutoXplainR permutation importance> #>   metric: rmse | repeats: 5 | baseline: 2.5436 #>  feature importance std_error  conf_low conf_high sign_stability #>   engine   2.872314 0.5134143 1.4468471   4.29778              1 #>   weight   1.382660 0.1783360 0.8875198   1.87780              1 #>   intervals describe permutation Monte Carlo variation, not population inference

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: Partial dependence replaces one feature leaving others unchanged. dependent predictors, can ask model score combinations absent reference data. Accumulated local effects (ALE) instead average local prediction differences within observed feature bins (Apley Zhu, 2020). effect outputs include relative empirical support. uncertainty columns remain descriptive computation diagnostics.

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: near-optimal indicator relative models supplied call. claim enumerate every near-optimal model hypothesis class. near-optimal supplied models disagree, audit recommends reporting importance range rather single-model ranking. main components ordinary data structures: Evidence grades control wording report permits: : stable marginal evidence configured diagnostics; B: usable stated uncertainty; C: sensitivity finding ; D: make feature claim. cutoffs visible source configured justified application. grade turn associational explanation causal one.
code: audit <- audit_explanations(   list(explainer_a, explainer_b),   features = c("wt", "hp", "disp", "qsec"),   n_repeats = 10,   performance_tolerance = 0.10,   dependence_threshold = 0.70,   seed = 2026 )  audit #> <AutoXplainR explanation evidence audit> #>   grade:              D (diagnostic, not certification) #>   models:             2 (2 near-optimal) #>   stable claims:      0.0% #>   max dependence:     0.929 #>   explanation accord: 0.6 #>   prediction accord:  1 #>  #> Findings #>   [warning] 4 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #>   [warning] 8 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported. #>   [critical] Near-optimal models disagree on the feature-importance ranking. #>   [note] The permutation budget is suitable for smoke testing, not a final report. audit$performance #>                                 model    score metric near_optimal relative_gap #> displacement model displacement model 2.543600   rmse         TRUE   0.05433181 #> acceleration model acceleration model 2.412523   rmse         TRUE   0.00000000 audit$dependence #>   feature max_association associated_feature high_dependence #> 1      wt       0.9285714               disp            TRUE #> 2      hp       0.8024096               disp            TRUE #> 3    disp       0.9285714                 wt            TRUE #> 4    qsec       0.7784571                 hp            TRUE audit$explanation_agreement$importance_ranges #>      feature min_importance max_importance mean_importance #> wt        wt     0.98920102       2.053720      1.52146039 #> hp        hp     1.70166680       2.555675      2.12867108 #> disp    disp    -0.12627171       0.000000     -0.06313585 #> qsec    qsec    -0.09100309       0.000000     -0.04550155 audit$findings #>   severity                        code #> 1  warning          feature_dependence #> 2  warning limited_importance_evidence #> 3 critical       rashomon_disagreement #> 4     note      low_monte_carlo_budget #>                                                                     message #> 1                             4 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #> 2                    8 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported. #> 3           Near-optimal models disagree on the feature-importance ranking. #> 4 The permutation budget is suitable for smoke testing, not a final report. #>                                                                                          evidence #> 1                                                          Maximum observed association is 0.929. #> 2 4 interval(s) include zero; 2 have sign stability below 0.8; 8 exceed the dependence threshold. #> 3                                                                    Mean Spearman agreement: 0.6 #> 4                                                              10 repeats per model-feature pair. #>                                                                                                                                recommendation #> 1                          Prefer ALE for effects; treat marginal PFI as model reliance, and use a conditional method for conditional claims. #> 2 Inspect repeat distributions and dependence, increase evaluation data when uncertainty is material, and avoid ranking unsupported features. #> 3                                              Report model-class importance ranges instead of presenting one best model's ranking as unique. #> 4                                                             Use at least 20 repeats for routine work and more when feature ranks are close.

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: report standalone HTML file findings, action items, model feature tables, interpretation boundaries, configuration, timestamps, explainer IDs. Store model version, evaluation-data version, analysis code.
code: path <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") render_explanation_report(audit, path) file.exists(path) #> [1] TRUE unlink(path)

title: Auditing Explanation Evidence with AutoXplainR
text: explanation core require H2O Java. H2O useful fitting candidate set, keep boundary: Held-test data passed H2O validation frame default. Numeric two-level outcomes classified correctly, training preprocessing applied evaluation data fitted recipe.
code: result <- autoxplain(   training_data,   target_column = "outcome",   test_data = held_out_data,   max_models = 10,   max_runtime_secs = 300,   seed = 2026 )  explainers <- as_explainers(result) audit <- audit_explanations(explainers) generate_dashboard(result, "automl-evidence.html")

text: local narrative deterministic. Remote Gemini generation explicit optional boundary receives aggregated diagnostics. prompt forbids causal, safety, fairness, compliance claims, generated text still requires human review.
code: memo <- generate_natural_language_report(audit, use_remote = FALSE) cat(substr(memo, 1, 300), "...") #> # Explanation Evidence Report #>  #> ## Scope #> This is a descriptive summary of a unspecified task for `unspecified`, covering 2 model(s) and 4 feature(s). #>  #> ## Reliability first #> The heuristic explanation-evidence grade is **D**; this is a diagnostic, not a certification. #> The stable-claim rate is 0.0%. #>  #> ##  ...

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: Stress-test evidence behind model explanations. explainability tools calculate importance ranking effect curve. AutoXplainR asks next question: resulting claim stable, supported data, consistent across similarly performing models? package wraps fitted tabular models behind one prediction contract, combines repeated permutation importance, Monte Carlo stability, feature dependence, accumulated local effects (ALE), predictive multiplicity, near-optimal-model explanation disagreement one auditable report. AutoXplainR diagnostic software. grades transparent heuristics, causal evidence, statistical certification, fairness validation, safety approval, regulatory compliance.

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: polished feature-importance chart can conceal several different problems: ranking changes permutation repeated; correlated predictors make marginal permutations PDP combinations implausible; several models perform equally well rely different features; interval across random permutations mistaken population inference; generated narrative overstates numerical analysis established. AutoXplainR makes failure modes first-class outputs. distinctive unit work plot—explanation evidence audit findings, permitted-claim labels, configuration, provenance.

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: AutoXplainR active development yet CRAN. model-agnostic core lightweight runtime dependency set. H2O AutoML, Plotly charts, remote Gemini narratives optional integrations.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Matt17BR/autoXplainR")

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: Use genuinely held-data explanations whenever possible. audit contains: baseline held-performance supplied near-optimal model set; repeat-level permutation scores, Monte Carlo intervals, sign stability; pairwise feature-dependence warnings; importance ranges rank agreement across near-optimal models; prediction agreement ambiguity diagnostics; prioritized findings concrete next action; package version, configuration, seed, model labels, explainer IDs.
code: library(AutoXplainR)  train <- mtcars[1:24, ] test  <- mtcars[25:32, ]  model_a <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = train) model_b <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = train)  explainer_a <- explain_model(   model_a,   test,   y = "mpg",   label = "weight + power + displacement",   metadata = list(evaluation_role = "test") )  explainer_b <- explain_model(   model_b,   test,   y = "mpg",   label = "weight + power + acceleration",   metadata = list(evaluation_role = "test") )  audit <- audit_explanations(   list(explainer_a, explainer_b),   n_repeats = 30,   performance_tolerance = 0.05,   seed = 2026 )  audit render_explanation_report(audit, "explanation-evidence.html")

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: ALE default effect estimator marginal PDPs can extrapolate unrealistic combinations predictors dependent. PDP remains available compatibility returns relative support plus dependence warning. plotted intervals descriptive computation-level diagnostics, population confidence intervals.
code: ale <- explain_effect(explainer_a, feature = "wt") pdp <- explain_effect(explainer_a, feature = "wt", method = "pdp")  ale attr(pdp, "dependence_warning")

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: unsupported model classes, provide function signature function(model, newdata) function(newdata): Regression functions return numeric vector. Binary classifiers return positive-class probability. Multiclass classifiers return named probability matrix one column per outcome class. AutoXplainR validates contract starting audit.
code: explainer <- explain_model(   model = fitted_object,   data = held_out_data,   y = "outcome",   task = "binary",   positive = "yes",   predict_function = function(model, newdata) {     # Return P(outcome == "yes") as a numeric vector.   } )

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: autoxplain() now adapter, package architecture. treats numeric two-level outcome classification, keeps held-test data separate H2O validation default, applies training preprocessing recipe evaluation data, records full fitting contract. Potentially destructive preprocessing identifier removal guessed ordinal conversion opt-. Every applied transformation returned reusable recipe.
code: # install.packages("h2o") result <- autoxplain(   training_data,   target_column = "outcome",   test_data = test_data,   max_models = 10,   max_runtime_secs = 300,   seed = 2026 )  explainers <- as_explainers(result) audit <- audit_explanations(explainers) generate_dashboard(result, "automl-evidence.html")

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: local deterministic narrative complete without network call. remote Gemini narrative attempted use_remote = TRUE API key available. aggregated diagnostics sent—never raw rows, model objects, case-level predictions, key . prompt forbids causal, fairness, safety, compliance claims requires limitations precede rankings. numerical audit remains authoritative.
code: local_memo <- generate_natural_language_report(audit, use_remote = FALSE)  # Explicit remote opt-in: Sys.setenv(GEMINI_API_KEY = "...") remote_memo <- generate_natural_language_report(audit, use_remote = TRUE)

title: Stress-Testing Evidence Behind Model Explanations
text: Permutation importance estimates fitted-model reliance chosen evaluation distribution. Correlated predictors can share mask reliance. default repeat interval captures random-permutation variation. Repeating permutation create independent observations population. “near-optimal set” contains models supplied audit empirical approximation, enumeration full Rashomon set. ALE summarizes local prediction differences within observed feature bins. safer PDP dependence, still associational. Negative importance values retained. can reflect noise, finite data, model misspecification, genuinely harmful predictor. population inference conditional feature importance, pair package method designed estimand (example, xplainfi) record result audit metadata.

H2O AutoML, Plotly, remote narrative generation optional integrations rather package startup requirements.

what: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: audit_explanations() AutoXplainR's primary workflow. evaluates repeated permutation importance, feature dependence, Monte Carlo stability, prediction disagreement, explanation disagreement among near-equivalent models. output distinguishes robust descriptive evidence claims need data conditional method.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
code: audit_explanations(   explainers,   features = NULL,   metric = "auto",   n_repeats = 20L,   seed = 123L,   confidence = 0.95,   performance_tolerance = 0.05,   dependence_threshold = 0.7 )

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: explainers autoxplain_explainer list explainers. features Features shared every explainer. Defaults intersection. metric Performance metric passed calculate_permutation_importance(). n_repeats Number permutations per model feature. seed Reproducible seed. confidence Monte Carlo interval level. performance_tolerance Relative tolerance defining empirical set near-optimal supplied models. example, 0.05 retains models whose evaluation score within five percent best supplied score. dependence_threshold Association marginal importance PDP claims receive dependence warning.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: object class autoxplain_audit.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
text: diagnostic protocol, formal certification substitute domain review, causal identification, external validation.

title: Stress-test the evidence behind model explanations — audit_explanations
code: train <- mtcars[1:24, ] test <- mtcars[25:32, ] lm1 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, train) lm2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + qsec, train) e1 <- explain_model(lm1, test, "mpg", label = "model A") e2 <- explain_model(lm2, test, "mpg", label = "model B") audit <- audit_explanations(list(e1, e2), n_repeats = 5) audit #> <AutoXplainR explanation evidence audit> #>   grade:              C (diagnostic, not certification) #>   models:             2 (1 near-optimal) #>   stable claims:      0.0% #>   max dependence:     0.929 #>   explanation accord: n/a (one model) #>   prediction accord:  n/a (one model) #>  #> Findings #>   [warning] 8 feature(s) exceed the dependence threshold. #>   [warning] 20 model-feature claim(s) are qualified or unsupported. #>   [note] The permutation budget is suitable for smoke testing, not a final report. #>   [note] The audit cannot verify that explanation data are independent of model fitting.

what: Fit a reproducible H2O AutoML candidate set — autoxplain
title: Fit a reproducible H2O AutoML candidate set — autoxplain
text: autoxplain() optional model-fitting convenience layer. core explanation audit API require H2O; users can call explain_model() fitted model.

title: Fit a reproducible H2O AutoML candidate set — autoxplain
code: autoxplain(   data,   target_column,   max_models = 10L,   max_runtime_secs = 300L,   seed = 123L,   test_data = NULL,   enable_preprocessing = TRUE,   preprocessing_config = list(),   task = c("auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass"),   nfolds = 5L,   sort_metric = "AUTO",   include_algos = NULL,   exclude_algos = NULL,   use_test_as_validation = FALSE,   init_h2o = TRUE,   h2o_nthreads = -1L,   h2o_max_mem_size = "2G",   verbosity = c("quiet", "info") )

title: Fit a reproducible H2O AutoML candidate set — autoxplain
text: data Training data frame. target_column Name outcome column. max_models Maximum number base models. max_runtime_secs Overall training time budget seconds. seed Reproducible H2O seed. test_data Optional held-evaluation data. used H2O validation frame unless use_test_as_validation = TRUE. enable_preprocessing Apply preprocess_for_h2o(). preprocessing_config Named overrides preprocessing. Identifier removal defaults FALSE. task One "auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass". nfolds Number H2O cross-validation folds. least two folds required stacked ensembles desired. sort_metric H2O AutoML leaderboard metric. include_algos, exclude_algos Optional H2O algorithm filters. Supply one. use_test_as_validation Whether pass test_data H2O validation frame. default keeps held-explanation data independent. init_h2o Start local H2O cluster connection available. h2o_nthreads Threads used starting H2O. h2o_max_mem_size Memory used starting H2O. verbosity One "quiet" "info".

title: Fit a reproducible H2O AutoML candidate set — autoxplain
text: autoxplain_result containing models, data-frame leaderboard, task metadata, preprocessing provenance, evaluation data.

Defaults FALSE; unique values alone never trigger removal. missing_value_strategy One "keep", "drop_rows", "drop_columns", "impute". Legacy names "remove_rows" "remove_columns" accepted. missing_column_threshold Fraction missing drop_columns removes predictor. verbose Emit concise progress messages. handle_missing, convert_characters, remove_id_columns Deprecated logical aliases retained compatibility.

title: Render a standalone explanation evidence report — render_explanation_report
code: fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars) x <- explain_model(fit, mtcars, "mpg") audit <- audit_explanations(x, n_repeats = 3) path <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") render_explanation_report(audit, path) unlink(path)

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Repositioned AutoXplainR H2O-specific dashboard generator model-agnostic explanation evidence auditor. Added explain_model() validated prediction contract regression, binary classification, multiclass classification. Added audit_explanations() combine repeated-importance stability, feature dependence, near-optimal-model importance ranges, prediction agreement, prioritized findings, explicit permitted-claim labels. Added render_explanation_report(), standalone accessible HTML artifact runtime dependency H2O, Plotly, Flexdashboard, LLM.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Rebuilt permutation importance retain repeat-level values, sign stability, grouped-feature permutations, optional blocked permutations, Monte Carlo intervals whose interpretation stated explicitly. Added accumulated local effects (ALE) default feature-effect method. Rebuilt PDP estimation relative support predictor-dependence diagnostics. Added supplied-model Rashomon diagnostics: empirical near-optimal membership, feature-importance ranges, rank agreement, prediction ambiguity. Numeric two-level outcomes now detected binary classification instead silently becoming regression targets.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: H2O AutoML now optional fitting adapter; core audits use lightweight runtime dependency set. Test data remain held H2O validation default. Preprocessing conservative, returns reusable recipe, applies fitted levels/imputation values evaluation data. Identifier removal guessed ordinal conversion opt-. Replaced generated 2,300-line Flexdashboard implementation evidence-report pipeline used model-agnostic callers. Replaced fixed, ranking-first LLM prompts local deterministic reports optional aggregated-data-Gemini narrative. Removed bundled third-party datasets whose provenance redistribution terms documented.

title: AutoXplainR 0.2.0
text: Added CRAN-oriented metadata, automated checks, coverage, pkgdown deployment, issue forms, contribution security policies, citation metadata, release checklist. Split fast model-agnostic unit tests opt-H2O integration tests. Rebuilt examples vignette around held-evaluation explicit interpretation boundaries.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.2
text: Added min-max weighted model score. Added performance_weight legacy dashboard.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.1
text: Expanded legacy dashboard Gemini integration.

title: AutoXplainR 0.1.0
text: Initial experimental H2O AutoML release.
